LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER JOEL SINGER'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE REVOCATION IS UNREASONABLE AND GROSSLY EXCESSIVE. THE CASE IS REMANDED BACK TO THE MEDICAL BOARD FOR A FURTHER DECISION IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S FINDING IN TODAY'S HEARING. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEE IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Ntc And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-09-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHLEEN MORGAN, TEL. NO. 994-1342 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. JANE ZACK SIMON, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5544 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER JOEL SINGER'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate is Granted. The court finds that the revocation is unreasonable and grossly excessive. The case is remanded back to the Medical Board for a further decision in light of the court's finding in today's hearing. Request for attorney's fee is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Ntc And Motion For Writ Of Mandate

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER JOEL SINGER's Motion For Writ Of Mandate is continued to July 9, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 on the Court's own motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
